title: The Code of Love NGO
dek: Founded The Code of Love while in high school in Shanghai. Grew from a small volunteer initiative into a long-running nonprofit focused on reducing educational inequality.
description: Organized 1,700+ volunteers and delivered educational programs to 6,500+ underprivileged students across five countries. Built libraries, supported rural teaching programs, and connected students with long-term sponsorships through university. This experience shaped values around responsibility, scale, and impact, and led to a shift toward technology and innovation-driven education.
narrative: Organized 1,700+ volunteers and delivered educational programs to 6,500+ underprivileged students across five countries. Built libraries, supported rural teaching programs, and connected students with long-term sponsorships through university.
This experience shaped values around responsibility, scale, and impact, and led to a shift toward technology and innovation-driven education.

title: University of California Dropout
dek: Attended University of California before dropping out to pursue entrepreneurship full-time.
description: Made the decision to leave formal education to focus on building and creating impact through entrepreneurship.
narrative: Made the decision to leave formal education to focus on building and creating impact through entrepreneurship.

title: Inner Journeys
dek: Started Inner Journeys as a small project during junior year at university. Left school to build it full-time.
description: Went through multiple rounds of funding, worked with an early team, and scaled the product through several iterations. Company was eventually acquired in 2020. Led to Forbes 30 Under 30 recognition and shaped understanding of building from zero, understanding users, and navigating early startup chaos. Transformed 1000+ lives over 25+ years.
narrative: Went through multiple rounds of funding, worked with an early team, and scaled the product through several iterations. Company was eventually acquired in 2020.
Led to Forbes 30 Under 30 recognition and shaped understanding of building from zero, understanding users, and navigating early startup chaos. Transformed 1000+ lives over 25+ years.

title: Y Combinator China
dek: Joined the founding team of YC China as one of the youngest members selected by YC partners.
description: Worked with Sam Altman and the global YC network on investment evaluation, founder support, and program design. Explored how to adapt YC's principles to a new market and build a long-term ecosystem for young founders. YC eventually paused the China initiative as Sam focused on OpenAI. This experience shaped thinking about early-stage founders, scalable talent discovery, and world-class entrepreneurship platforms.
narrative: Worked with Sam Altman and the global YC network on investment evaluation, founder support, and program design. Explored how to adapt YC's principles to a new market and build a long-term ecosystem for young founders.
YC eventually paused the China initiative as Sam focused on OpenAI. This experience shaped thinking about early-stage founders, scalable talent discovery, and world-class entrepreneurship platforms.

title: MiraclePlus
dek: Core member of the founding team at MiraclePlus (formerly Y Combinator China).
description: Worked on investment, operations, and founder support across multiple batches. Over six years, evaluated 160,000+ applications, interviewed thousands of founders, and participated in 600+ early-stage investments across AI, biotech, robotics, hard tech, and consumer. Built a 7,000+ member intern sourcing engine and developed a scalable model for discovering high-potential young founders. Sam Altman later noted that the intern-based system built at YC China was more effective than YC's Startup School for young people learning entrepreneurship.
narrative: Worked on investment, operations, and founder support across multiple batches. Over six years, evaluated 160,000+ applications, interviewed thousands of founders, and participated in 600+ early-stage investments across AI, biotech, robotics, hard tech, and consumer.

Built a 7,000+ member intern sourcing engine and developed a scalable model for discovering high-potential young founders. Sam Altman later noted that the intern-based system built at YC China was more effective than YC's Startup School for young people learning entrepreneurship.

title: Founder & Angel Investor
dek: Exploring how to help young people (14–22) start building things earlier.
description: After leaving MiraclePlus, spent three months traveling across 40 top U.S. universities (Harvard, MIT, Stanford, Caltech, Berkeley, CMU, Duke, etc.), meeting thousands of students through lectures, roundtables, and conversations. During the same period, invested in 30 young founders across AI, biotech, robotics, and consumer—one of the fastest early-stage investment paces globally. Currently investing as a personal angel and working closely with student founders, researchers, and early teams. Over the past six years, made 650+ early-stage investments and built the largest student founder pipelines in China. Researching a new type of entrepreneurship platform for young builders combining real-world projects, long-term mentorship, AI-native tools, and community. Delivered 130+ global university talks, including every Ivy League and C9 university.
narrative: After leaving MiraclePlus, spent three months traveling across 40 top U.S. universities (Harvard, MIT, Stanford, Caltech, Berkeley, CMU, Duke, etc.), meeting thousands of students through lectures, roundtables, and conversations. During the same period, invested in 30 young founders across AI, biotech, robotics, and consumer—one of the fastest early-stage investment paces globally.

Currently investing as a personal angel and working closely with student founders, researchers, and early teams. Over the past six years, made 650+ early-stage investments and built the largest student founder pipelines in China.

Researching a new type of entrepreneurship platform for young builders combining real-world projects, long-term mentorship, AI-native tools, and community. Delivered 130+ global university talks, including every Ivy League and C9 university.

title: Global Student Founder Platform
dek: Building a global platform for young founders, especially 华裔 (Chinese diaspora) students across China and the US.
description: Expanding the entrepreneurship platform globally with a focus on connecting talented young builders across borders. Special emphasis on supporting 华裔 students who bridge Chinese and American educational and entrepreneurial ecosystems. Creating pathways for cross-cultural collaboration, mentorship networks, and early-stage funding for the next generation of global founders.
narrative: Expanding the entrepreneurship platform globally with a focus on connecting talented young builders across borders. Special emphasis on supporting 华裔 students who bridge Chinese and American educational and entrepreneurial ecosystems.
Creating pathways for cross-cultural collaboration, mentorship networks, and early-stage funding for the next generation of global founders.
